question.text: What role did the implementation of the Companies Act 2013 play in relation to proxy advisors?

answer.text: It prohibited proxy advisory services in India
It established SEBI as the sole regulator of proxy advisors
Along with enhanced SEBI LODR corporate governance standards, it made the role of proxy advisers more prominent
It mandated every listed company to appoint a proxy advisor

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Since the implementation of the Companies Act 2013 and enhanced corporate governance standards under SEBI LODR Regulations 2015, the role of proxy advisers has become more prominent. **The Trap:** The Act did not prohibit or mandate proxy advisors; it created an environment where their role became more relevant. **Cross-Exam Context:** Understanding the regulatory evolution of corporate governance in India. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The interplay between Companies Act 2013 and SEBI LODR 2015 enhanced the need for proxy advisory services.
